messages: Channeling my inner Morgan Drinks Coffee...
Asking the beans how they feel...
Running through the Barista Hustle curriculum...
Measuring TDS for the nth time...
Channeling is bad, unless it's channeling vibes...
Watching one more Weiss Distribution video...
Preheating the group head of knowledge...
Tasting notes: patience, with hints of anticipation...

errors.uploadImage: Please upload an image file (JPG, PNG, etc.)

errors.generateFailedGeneric: Failed to generate profile. Please check your connection and try again.
errors.generateFailedNetwork: Connection lost during profile generation. Check your network and try again.
errors.generateFailed404Route: Failed to generate profile: The server route was not found (404). This is often a reverse-proxy/API path mismatch: verify your proxy forwards /api/* to MeticAI.
errors.generateFailedTimeout: Failed to generate profile: The AI request timed out. Please retry; if this repeats, reduce prompt complexity or try a stronger Gemini model.
errors.generateFailedValidation: Failed to generate profile: The AI generated an invalid profile schema. Please retry; a second attempt often succeeds.
errors.generateBusy: A profile is already being generated. Your form has been preserved; please try again in a few minutes.
errors.saveFailed: Failed to save results. Please try again.
errors.aiDisabled: AI profile generation is currently disabled. Enable AI in Settings and ensure an API key is configured.

settings.title: Settings
settings.aboutDescription1: When I got my Meticulous, after a loooong wait, I was overwhelmed with the options - dialing in was no longer just adjusting grind size, the potential was (and is) basically limitless - my knowledge and time not so.
settings.aboutDescription2: This, "MeticAI", is a growing set of AI tools to enable me, and you, to get the most out of a Meticulous Espresso machine. Among other things it lets you automatically create espresso profiles tailored to your preferences and coffee at hand, understand your espresso profiles and shot data like never before, and ultimately: lets you unleash your Meticulous.
settings.apiKeyConfigured: API key is configured. Click the field to enter a new key.
settings.apiKeyConfiguredExtended: API key is configured. Click the field to enter a new key. You can keep the key saved and disable AI features below.
settings.biometricReasonApiKey: Authenticate to reveal your API key
settings.aiAssistantDescription: Gemini API key is optional. Add one only if you want AI-powered profile and analysis features.
settings.enableAiFeaturesDescription: Turns AI tools on/off without removing your saved API key.
settings.hideAiWhenUnavailableDescription: When disabled, AI controls stay visible but disabled until AI is available.
settings.meticulousIpDescription: The IP address of your Meticulous espresso machine on your local network

espressoCompass.title: Espresso Compass
espressoCompass.subtitle: Select the flavors you're tasting to get dialing-in advice.
espressoCompass.ruleStartSimple: Start simple. Begin with a built-in Meticulous profile (like "Italian"). Master dose, yield, and temperature first — these are the biggest levers. Advanced flow and pressure control is powerful, but works best when the basics are dialed.
espressoCompass.ruleDontChangeDose: When adjusting yield (final weight), don't change the dose in your portafilter; only change the profile.
espressoCompass.ruleYieldTime: Changing yield will inherently change your shot time. Adjust grind only if your shot time is wildly off target. For flavor direction changes, profile adjustments are more effective than grind alone.
espressoCompass.ruleEvenExtraction: A perfectly even extraction creates a larger "sweet spot" to work within: focus on puck prep first.
espressoCompass.rulePreciseControl: Steer flavor with flow, pressure, and temperature. Once your basics are dialed, explore the Meticulous's precision: higher flow pushes toward acidity and clarity (great for light roasts), lower flow toward body and sweetness. Higher temperature extracts more (light roasts need 92–96°C), lower temperature prevents bitterness (dark roasts: 82–90°C). Tapering pressure in the final third reduces harshness.
espressoCompass.attribution: Conceptually inspired by the Espresso Compass by Barista Hustle. Adapted for the Meticulous espresso machine.
espressoCompass.emptyState: Select flavors above to analyze your shot and get dialing-in advice.

analysis.sweetSpotDesc: You're right in the sweet spot! The extraction and strength are well-balanced.
analysis.sweetSpotAdvice: Any further changes are up to personal preference. Try small tweaks: ±0.5°C temperature or ±1g yield - to explore the flavors of your coffee.
analysis.underDesc: The shot is under-extracted: too much acidity is coming through before the sweet and balanced compounds can dissolve.
analysis.underAdvice: Increase the final weight/yield of your profile to allow more water through the puck. You can also try increasing temperature by 1–2°C. If extraction is severely low, grind finer.
analysis.overDesc: The espresso is over-extracted: bitter, dry, and harsh compounds from late in the extraction are dominating.
analysis.overAdvice: Reduce the final weight/yield of your profile so the shot ends earlier. You can also try decreasing temperature by 1–2°C. If severely over-extracted, grind coarser.
analysis.strongDesc: The shot is very dense and intense: the concentration is high.
analysis.strongAdvice: Increase the final weight of your profile so more water passes through, diluting the concentration. This shifts the brew ratio toward a longer shot.
analysis.weakDesc: The shot feels thin or watery: it lacks the syrupy body expected from espresso.
analysis.weakAdvice: Decrease the final weight of your profile so less water passes through, concentrating the flavors. You can also slightly increase the dose.
analysis.unevenDesc: You're tasting both sourness and bitterness simultaneously: a classic sign of channeling (water finding paths of least resistance through the puck).
analysis.unevenAdvice: Focus on puck preparation before changing your profile. Use a WDT tool to distribute grounds evenly, and ensure a perfectly level tamp. On the Meticulous, also verify that the pre-infusion stage has enough time to saturate the puck evenly.
